vision.one_line: A static public home for Viggo Meesters' projects, systems, guides, and proof-first working style.
vision.north_star: A visitor can understand what Viggo builds, how he works, and which public artifacts are worth opening within a few clicks.
vision.promise: A fresh clone should explain the site, validate all public pages locally, and preserve the boundary between public narrative and private vault context.

principles.title: Agent-readable contracts
principles.rule: Keep workflow and review context in explicit files that agents can validate without private chat history.

principles.title: JSON-first machine contracts
principles.rule: Machine-consumable workflow state should be JSON or JSONL, with Markdown used for public narrative only.
bad: prose as the only source of truth

principles.title: Static first
principles.rule: Keep the public site as plain static files unless a concrete repeated maintenance problem justifies tooling.

principles.title: Public-safe by default
principles.rule: Only publish content that is safe for a public GitHub repository and public website.

principles.title: Proof-first delivery
principles.rule: Every meaningful site or repo change should be backed by local checks and, when visual, browser evidence.

principles.title: Repo-local agent continuity
principles.rule: The repository owns its `.go/` workflow state so future agents can continue without private chat or vault state.
bad: hidden central execution state as the only source
manual task JSON when CLI primitives exist

non_goals: Replacing the static site with a framework by default
Treating generated process notes as visitor-facing copy
Making the repository depend on private local paths

required_boundaries: explicit source or freshness labels when relevant

acceptance_scorecard.question: Can a fresh clone validate the public site?
acceptance_scorecard.pass_condition: npm run check passes without private dependencies.

acceptance_scorecard.question: Can an agent read direction and next work locally?
Is the repo safe and understandable as a public GitHub project?
acceptance_scorecard.pass_condition: README, license, security, changelog, metadata, and privacy review are present.

review_checklist: Update sitemap/canonical URLs for new public pages
Keep private operational details out of public files
Report any privacy false positives explicitly before publishing

maturity_model.meaning: The static site and repo-local state exist.
Local site checks and repo-local checks pass.
GitHub metadata, docs, release, and privacy review are complete.

residual_risk_wording: This repository is public-facing. High-level descriptions of personal systems are intentional; raw private operational data is not.
